name: The Fool
numerology: 0 — pure potential, the open space before beginnings
keywords_upright: new beginnings; spontaneity; innocence; leap of faith; free spirit
keywords_reversed: recklessness; hesitation; naivety; poor judgment; fear of change
meaning_upright: A fresh start taken with an open heart, stepping into the unknown with trust rather than a detailed plan.
meaning_reversed: Either a reckless leap taken without thought or a journey postponed out of fear.
love: A fresh romantic chapter, playful energy, and following the heart without guarantees.
career: A new venture, role, or idea that rewards courage and an open mind over rigid planning.
mood: Light, curious, and playful — an openness that welcomes surprises rather than bracing against them.
spiritual: Beginner's mind: trusting the journey before the map exists and treating the unknown as sacred ground.

name: The Magician
keywords_upright: manifestation; willpower; resourcefulness; skill; focused action
keywords_reversed: manipulation; untapped talent; scattered energy; trickery; illusion
meaning_upright: That every tool needed is already on the table; it is a card of manifestation through focused will and skill.
meaning_reversed: Scattered focus, wasted talent, or someone using charm and cleverness to manipulate.
love: Attraction built on confidence and communication, and the power to actively create the relationship you want.
career: Skill meeting opportunity — a strong moment to pitch, launch, or negotiate.
mood: Confident and energized — a focused spark that makes things feel possible right now.
spiritual: Alignment: bringing thought, word, and action into one channel so intention becomes creation.

name: The High Priestess
keywords_upright: intuition; inner wisdom; mystery; the subconscious; stillness
keywords_reversed: ignored intuition; secrets; disconnection from self; hidden agendas; surface knowledge
meaning_upright: Stillness and listening; the answer is already known at an intuitive level even if it cannot yet be explained.
meaning_reversed: Intuition being drowned out by noise, other people's opinions, or a refusal to look inward.
love: Unspoken feelings and undercurrents — trust what is sensed rather than only what is said.
career: Watching and listening before acting; information is still hidden and patience reveals it.
mood: Quiet, inward, and softly mysterious — a day for listening more than speaking.
spiritual: The inner voice: intuition, dreams, and knowledge that arrives in stillness rather than study.

name: The Empress
keywords_upright: abundance; nurturing; fertility; creativity; sensuality
keywords_reversed: creative block; smothering; self-neglect; dependence; stagnation
meaning_upright: Abundance, nurture, and creative fertility — things growing because they are cared for.
meaning_reversed: Creative block, self-neglect, or care that has tipped into smothering or dependence.
love: The Empress is one of the warmest cards, indicating deepening affection, nurturing partnership, and sensual connection.
career: Creative projects and steady cultivation — what is tended now will bear fruit.
mood: Warm, sensual, and content — comfort in the body and pleasure in simple things.
spiritual: Abundance: creation flows through care, and tending something gently is itself a devotion.

name: The Emperor
keywords_upright: structure; authority; stability; leadership; discipline
keywords_reversed: rigidity; control issues; domination; lack of discipline; abuse of power
meaning_upright: Structure, boundaries, and earned authority — building something stable through discipline and clear rules.
meaning_reversed: Control hardening into rigidity or domination, or conversely a lack of structure that lets things drift.
love: A stabilizing, protective presence, though it cautions against controlling behavior replacing warmth.
career: Leadership, order, and long-term building — take responsibility and set the framework.
mood: Steady, resolute, and composed — feet planted, emotions in order.
spiritual: Discipline as devotion: structure and healthy boundaries as ways of honoring what matters.

name: The Hierophant
keywords_upright: tradition; guidance; shared values; institutions; mentorship
keywords_reversed: rebellion; questioning convention; restriction; dogma; breaking rules
meaning_upright: Tradition, teachers, and the wisdom of established paths — learning from those who walked the road before.
meaning_reversed: Chafing against convention — rules, institutions, or expectations that no longer fit.
love: Commitment and shared values, sometimes literally marriage or formalizing a bond.
career: Established institutions, mentorship, and proven methods over improvisation.
mood: Reflective and respectful — drawn toward counsel, study, or familiar ritual.
spiritual: Lineage: wisdom passed down through teachers, tradition, and shared practice.

name: The Lovers
keywords_upright: love; union; alignment; meaningful choice; harmony
keywords_reversed: disharmony; misalignment; avoidance of choice; temptation; imbalance
meaning_upright: Deep connection and alignment of hearts and values, and also of a significant choice made consciously.
meaning_reversed: Disharmony, mismatched values, or a choice being avoided.
love: Genuine union, mutual attraction, and a relationship built on real compatibility.
career: A values-based crossroads or a partnership that must be chosen deliberately.
mood: Tender and heart-open — connection feels close and choices feel meaningful.
spiritual: The Lovers is union and alignment: choosing from the soul so that love and values point the same way.

name: The Chariot
keywords_upright: determination; victory; willpower; self-control; momentum
keywords_reversed: lack of direction; aggression; obstacles; scattered forces; loss of control
meaning_upright: The Chariot is a card of victory through will — holding opposing forces together and driving them in one chosen direction.
meaning_reversed: Energy pulling in conflicting directions, forcing an outcome, or momentum stalling.
love: Pursuing the relationship with intention and overcoming distance or obstacles together.
career: Ambition rewarded — drive, focus, and control carry a project across the line.
mood: Driven and determined — momentum with hands firmly on the reins.
spiritual: The Chariot is mastery of opposites: holding conflicting forces together and steering them by will toward one aim.

name: Strength
keywords_upright: courage; inner strength; patience; compassion; gentle power
keywords_reversed: self-doubt; raw emotion; impatience; insecurity; forcing
meaning_upright: Power expressed as patience and compassion — taming the lion with a steady hand rather than a whip.
meaning_reversed: Self-doubt, depleted courage, or emotions running the show.
love: Patience, emotional honesty, and a bond that grows through gentleness rather than pressure.
career: Persistence and calm confidence over aggressive tactics — influence beats force.
mood: Calm courage — soft on the outside, unshakeable underneath.
spiritual: That gentleness is power: the inner beast is tamed by compassion, not force.

name: The Hermit
keywords_upright: introspection; solitude; inner guidance; wisdom; searching
keywords_reversed: isolation; loneliness; withdrawal; lost direction; avoidance
meaning_upright: The Hermit withdraws from noise to find the answer by his own lamp — a card of introspection, soul-searching, and hard-won wisdom.
meaning_reversed: Healthy solitude has slipped into isolation, or that reflection is being used to avoid life.
love: A period of reflection about what one truly needs, or a partner who requires space.
career: Research, deep work, and stepping back to reassess the path before advancing.
mood: Solitary and contemplative — a pull toward quiet and one's own company.
spiritual: The Hermit is the inner lamp: withdrawing from noise so the soul's own light can show the next step.

name: Wheel of Fortune
numerology: 10 — completion of a cycle; reduces to 1, a new beginning
keywords_upright: change; cycles; destiny; turning point; luck
keywords_reversed: resistance to change; bad luck; repeating cycles; setbacks; clinging
meaning_upright: The Wheel of Fortune marks a turning point: circumstances are shifting, often through forces beyond personal control.
meaning_reversed: The Wheel points to resisting a change that is happening anyway, or a cycle repeating because its lesson has not been learned.
love: The Wheel signals a destined shift — chance meetings, changing dynamics, or a relationship entering a new phase.
career: Changing circumstances and timely opportunities; stay adaptable and catch the upswing.
mood: Anticipatory and changeable — the sense that something is turning.
spiritual: The Wheel teaches cycles: seasons rise and fall by design, and trust means moving with the turn rather than against it.

name: Justice
numerology: 11 — master number of insight and balance; reduces to 2
keywords_upright: fairness; truth; accountability; cause and effect; clarity
keywords_reversed: unfairness; dishonesty; avoiding accountability; bias; imbalance
meaning_upright: Truth, fairness, and consequence: decisions and actions are being weighed, and outcomes will match what was earned.
meaning_reversed: Unfairness, self-deception, or dodging accountability.
love: Honesty and equal give-and-take; imbalances in effort or truth come due.
career: Contracts, negotiations, and fair dealing — integrity now determines the outcome.
mood: Sober and clear-eyed — weighing things honestly, including oneself.
spiritual: Justice is karma and truth: actions ripple back, and integrity is its own form of alignment.

name: The Hanged Man
keywords_upright: surrender; new perspective; pause; letting go; suspension
keywords_reversed: stalling; resistance; martyrdom; indecision; wasted sacrifice
meaning_upright: Willing pause and a change of perspective — progress right now comes from surrender, not pushing.
meaning_reversed: A pause that has become stalling, or sacrifice without purpose.
love: Patience and seeing the other person's viewpoint before acting.
career: Putting a plan on hold and re-examining assumptions — the delay itself is informative.
mood: Suspended and strangely peaceful — waiting that asks not to be rushed.
spiritual: The Hanged Man is surrender: releasing control long enough for a completely new perspective to arrive.

name: Death
numerology: 13 — reduces to 4: transformation building new structure
keywords_upright: endings; transformation; transition; release; renewal
keywords_reversed: resistance to endings; stagnation; fear of change; holding on; incomplete closure
meaning_upright: A definitive ending that clears space for transformation — a chapter closing so a truer one can begin.
meaning_reversed: An ending being resisted: clinging to what is already over and prolonging the discomfort of transition.
love: The end of a dynamic or relationship phase, making room for a more honest connection.
career: Closing a role, project, or identity — transformation follows the letting-go.
mood: Heavy yet quietly relieved — the weight of an ending already loosening its grip.
spiritual: Death is transformation: every rebirth begins with a release, and what falls away makes room for what is truly next.

name: Temperance
keywords_upright: balance; moderation; patience; blending; healing
keywords_reversed: excess; imbalance; impatience; friction; overcorrection
meaning_upright: Temperance blends opposites into harmony — a card of moderation, patience, and finding the middle path.
meaning_reversed: Excess or imbalance: too much of one thing, too little of another, and friction as a result.
love: Growing harmony, patience with differences, and a relationship finding its natural rhythm.
career: Steady progress, collaboration, and balancing competing demands without burning out.
mood: Serene and even — nothing extreme, everything gently blended.
spiritual: Temperance is alchemy: mixing opposites with patience until they become something finer than either alone.

name: The Devil
numerology: 15 — reduces to 6: harmony distorted by attachment
keywords_upright: attachment; temptation; unhealthy patterns; materialism; feeling trapped
keywords_reversed: breaking free; reclaiming power; awareness; release; recovery
meaning_upright: Chains that are worn voluntarily — addictions, obsessions, toxic dynamics, or fears that keep one bound.
meaning_reversed: The Devil is the moment of breaking free: seeing the pattern clearly and reclaiming power from it.
love: Intense attraction shadowed by possessiveness, dependency, or patterns that diminish both people.
career: Golden handcuffs, burnout loops, or compromising values for material security.
mood: Tense and restless — tempted, tethered, or circling an old habit.
spiritual: Shadow work: facing the attachments that bind and remembering the chains are loose.

name: The Tower
keywords_upright: sudden upheaval; revelation; collapse of the false; disruption; awakening
keywords_reversed: averted disaster; fear of change; delayed collapse; resisting the inevitable; internal upheaval
meaning_upright: The Tower is sudden upheaval that demolishes what was built on shaky ground — shocking, but ultimately clarifying.
meaning_reversed: Upheaval resisted, delayed, or happening internally — a quiet crisis of belief rather than an outer collapse.
love: A sudden revelation or shake-up that changes the relationship's foundation and demands honesty.
career: Abrupt disruption — restructures, failures, or truths surfacing — that clears the way for something sounder.
mood: Shaken and electric — the ground moving underfoot.
spiritual: The Tower is awakening by lightning: false structures fall so that truth has somewhere to stand.

name: The Star
numerology: 17 — reduces to 8: renewed strength and hope
keywords_upright: hope; renewal; healing; inspiration; faith
keywords_reversed: discouragement; lost faith; disconnection; self-doubt; dimmed hope
meaning_upright: The Star is the calm after the storm — hope, healing, and quiet faith that the path ahead is good.
meaning_reversed: Hope running low and inspiration feeling out of reach.
love: Healing after difficulty and renewed faith in connection — a gentle, promising sign.
career: Renewed purpose and long-term promise; keep faith in the direction even if progress is quiet.
mood: Hopeful and soothed — a quiet exhale after a long storm.
spiritual: The Star is grace: healing, renewal, and the faith that the universe is gently guiding, not testing.

name: The Moon
numerology: 18 — reduces to 9: completing the soul's night journey
keywords_upright: illusion; intuition; uncertainty; the subconscious; dreams
keywords_reversed: clarity emerging; released fear; truth surfacing; confusion lifting; self-deception ending
meaning_upright: The Moon casts a landscape of shadows where fear and imagination distort what is real — things are not as they appear.
meaning_reversed: Confusion beginning to lift: secrets surface, fears shrink to their true size, and the road becomes visible again.
love: Uncertainty, mixed signals, or projections — feelings are real but the picture is incomplete.
career: Unclear information or hidden factors; verify facts before committing.
mood: Dreamy and uneasy — feelings vivid, facts blurry.
spiritual: The Moon is the subconscious speaking: dreams, symbols, and intuition lighting a path the rational mind cannot.

name: The Sun
numerology: 19 — reduces to 1: a radiant new beginning
keywords_upright: joy; success; vitality; clarity; confidence
keywords_reversed: dimmed joy; temporary setbacks; pessimism; burnout; delayed success
meaning_upright: The Sun is the deck's most unambiguously positive card: warmth, vitality, success, and truth in full daylight.
meaning_reversed: The Sun does not turn dark — it dims: joy muted by pessimism, fatigue, or clouds of doubt over real success.
love: The Sun radiates happiness, openness, and celebration — a relationship thriving in honest light.
career: Visible success, recognition, and energy — momentum is real and deserved.
mood: Joyful and radiant — warmth, play, and easy confidence.
spiritual: The Sun is vitality and gratitude: clarity so bright that simply being alive feels like the blessing it is.

name: Judgement
keywords_upright: awakening; reckoning; renewal; calling; self-evaluation
keywords_reversed: self-criticism; ignoring the call; stuck in the past; harsh judgment; avoidance
meaning_upright: Judgement is the wake-up call: an honest reckoning with the past that frees one to rise into a new phase.
meaning_reversed: The call being ignored, or self-judgment so harsh it prevents moving forward.
love: Honest evaluation of the relationship's history and a chance at genuine renewal or closure.
career: A career reckoning or calling — an invitation to step into work that actually matters to you.
mood: Stirred and resolved — an honest reckoning that ends in relief.
spiritual: Judgement is the awakening call: rising from the past, forgiven and lighter, toward a truer purpose.

name: The World
numerology: 21 — reduces to 3: creative wholeness and completion
keywords_upright: completion; fulfillment; wholeness; achievement; integration
keywords_reversed: incompletion; loose ends; delayed closure; shortcuts; unfinished business
meaning_upright: The World is completion: a long cycle fulfilled, lessons integrated, and a well-earned sense of wholeness.
meaning_reversed: A goal almost reached but not closed — loose ends, missing steps, or closure being postponed.
love: A relationship reaching fulfillment and harmony, or closure that finally allows a new beginning.
career: The successful completion of a major goal and often expansion — travel, recognition, or a bigger stage.
mood: Fulfilled and celebratory — a deep sense of arrival.
spiritual: The World is integration: the lessons of a whole cycle woven into wholeness before the next dance begins.

name: Ace of Wands
keywords_upright: inspiration; new opportunity; creative spark; enthusiasm; potential
keywords_reversed: delays; false start; lack of motivation; blocked creativity; hesitation
meaning_upright: The Ace of Wands is a spark of pure creative potential — a new idea, passion, or opportunity bursting with energy.
meaning_reversed: The Ace shows inspiration that stalls: false starts, delays, or enthusiasm that fizzles before it becomes action.
love: A spark of fresh attraction or renewed passion in an existing bond.
career: The ignition of a new project or venture — promising, if acted on quickly.
mood: Excited and inspired — new energy crackling to be used.
spiritual: The Ace of Wands is the creative spark as gift: inspiration arriving from beyond and asking to be acted on.

name: Two of Wands
keywords_upright: planning; future vision; decision; expansion; the world in hand
keywords_reversed: fear of the unknown; playing it safe; poor planning; restlessness; indecision
meaning_upright: The Two of Wands is the planner's card: standing at a threshold, world in hand, weighing how far to venture.
meaning_reversed: Ambition trapped by fear of the unknown — staying in familiar territory while opportunity waits outside.
love: Weighing a decision about the relationship's future direction.
career: Strategic planning and considering expansion beyond the current comfort zone.
mood: Restless anticipation — plans forming, horizons calling.
spiritual: It expands the vision: the soul outgrowing familiar walls and daring to imagine a larger life.

name: Three of Wands
keywords_upright: progress; expansion; foresight; ships coming in; momentum
keywords_reversed: delays; obstacles to plans; limited vision; setbacks; impatience
meaning_upright: The Three of Wands shows plans in motion and horizons expanding — the ships are launched and beginning to return.
meaning_reversed: Delays and shipments that haven't come in: plans meeting friction or vision proving too narrow.
love: A relationship moving forward, sometimes across distance, with patience being rewarded.
career: Early results confirming a good strategy — think bigger and prepare for growth.
mood: Optimistic and forward-looking — effort already sailing back as results.
spiritual: Faithful patience: what was set in motion is growing even while it is out of sight.

name: Four of Wands
keywords_upright: celebration; homecoming; milestone; community; stability
keywords_reversed: instability at home; postponed celebration; transition; lack of support; shaky foundations
meaning_upright: The Four of Wands is celebration and homecoming — a milestone reached and shared with the people who matter.
meaning_reversed: Tension at home, a celebration delayed, or a sense of not quite belonging.
love: Happy milestones — moving in together, engagements, or simply a joyful, secure phase.
career: A completed stage worth celebrating and a supportive, stable team environment.
mood: Celebratory and secure — belonging, milestones, open doors.
spiritual: Gratitude in community: pausing to honor how far things have come, together.

name: Five of Wands
keywords_upright: conflict; competition; friction; clashing egos; scattered effort
keywords_reversed: avoiding conflict; resolution; inner conflict; release of tension; picking battles
meaning_upright: The Five of Wands is friction: competing voices, clashing egos, and energy scattered in a scramble.
meaning_reversed: Conflict either being dodged or finally winding down.
love: Bickering, rivalry, or outside interference creating unnecessary tension.
career: Competition and disagreement over direction — channel it into healthy rivalry, not chaos.
mood: Irritable and competitive — energy scattering into friction.
spiritual: Ego work: noticing the need to win and redirecting that fire into growth instead of noise.

name: Six of Wands
keywords_upright: victory; recognition; success; confidence; public acclaim
keywords_reversed: private victory; fall from grace; self-doubt; lack of recognition; ego inflation
meaning_upright: The Six of Wands is the victory parade: success achieved and publicly recognized.
meaning_reversed: Success unacknowledged, confidence wobbling, or acclaim that went to the head.
love: Pride in the relationship and mutual admiration, being 'seen' by a partner.
career: Recognition, promotion, or a win that raises your profile.
mood: Proud and uplifted — effort seen and cheered.
spiritual: It pairs success with humility: receiving recognition gracefully and honoring those who helped.

name: Seven of Wands
keywords_upright: defense; standing your ground; perseverance; conviction; holding advantage
keywords_reversed: overwhelm; giving up ground; exhaustion; defensiveness; yielding
meaning_upright: The Seven of Wands is the defense of hard-won ground: challengers press from below, but the higher position holds.
meaning_reversed: Defenses wearing thin: overwhelm, exhaustion, or surrendering ground that could have been held.
love: Protecting the relationship from outside pressure, or defending personal boundaries within it.
career: Defending your position, ideas, or territory against competition — persistence wins.
mood: Defensive but resolute — tested, yet standing firm.
spiritual: Conviction: defending what one truly believes, calmly, without needing everyone's agreement.

name: Eight of Wands
keywords_upright: swift movement; momentum; news arriving; alignment; rapid progress
keywords_reversed: delays; frustration; scattered energy; missed timing; slowdown
meaning_upright: The Eight of Wands is speed: events accelerating, messages arriving, and obstacles clearing all at once.
meaning_reversed: Delays and misfires: momentum stalling, messages crossed, timing off.
love: Fast-moving romance, important messages, or a relationship suddenly gaining momentum.
career: Rapid developments, quick decisions, and news that sets things in motion.
mood: Swift and eager — messages, movement, momentum.
spiritual: Flow: signs arriving quickly and the invitation to move with them rather than overthink.

name: Nine of Wands
keywords_upright: resilience; last stand; persistence; guardedness; nearly there
keywords_reversed: burnout; paranoia; giving up at the end; rigid defenses; battle fatigue
meaning_upright: The Nine of Wands is the wounded sentry still standing: battered by past battles but not beaten, with the finish line close.
meaning_reversed: Defenses built from old wounds doing more harm than protection, or collapse just short of the goal.
love: Guardedness from past hurt — the walls that protected once may now be keeping love out.
career: Fatigue near the end of a long effort; endurance through this last stretch pays off.
mood: Weary but determined — bruised, guarded, unbeaten.
spiritual: Resilience as practice: tending wounds without letting them close the heart before the last step.

name: Ten of Wands
numerology: 10 — completion of a cycle; reduces to 1, a new beginning
keywords_upright: burden; overload; responsibility; carrying too much; final effort
keywords_reversed: release; delegation; putting burdens down; collapse; learning to say no
meaning_upright: The Ten of Wands is success turned heavy: every wand carried alone, vision blocked by the load itself.
meaning_reversed: The moment of setting burdens down — delegating, quitting what isn't yours to carry, or collapsing under a load ignored too long.
love: One partner shouldering the relationship's weight alone — rebalancing is needed.
career: Overload and burnout risk; delegate and prioritize before the load drops you.
mood: Burdened and overloaded — carrying more than one's share.
spiritual: Release: learning that putting down what was never yours to carry is also an act of faith.

name: Page of Wands
numerology: Page — the student: curiosity, learning, messages
keywords_upright: enthusiasm; exploration; free spirit; exciting news; curiosity
keywords_reversed: scattered energy; hasty starts; boredom; unreliable news; restlessness
meaning_upright: The Page of Wands is the eager explorer: curious, spontaneous, and carrying news of exciting possibilities.
meaning_reversed: The Page starts everything and finishes nothing — enthusiasm without follow-through, or news that disappoints.
love: Flirtation, playful energy, or an admirer with an adventurous spirit.
career: A new idea or opportunity worth exploring with fresh eyes and low stakes.
mood: Curious and enthusiastic — sparks everywhere, ready to explore.
spiritual: Following inspiration: treating every spark of interest as a possible message from the soul.

name: Knight of Wands
numerology: Knight — the quester: action, pursuit, dedication
keywords_upright: action; adventure; passion; bold pursuit; charisma
keywords_reversed: impulsiveness; recklessness; scattered passion; delays in action; hot temper
meaning_upright: The Knight of Wands charges in with passion and flair — a card of bold, energetic pursuit of what excites.
meaning_reversed: The Knight is all gallop and no direction: impulsive moves, burned bridges, or passion that flames out.
love: Intense, fast-burning attraction or a charismatic pursuer — thrilling, but test the staying power.
career: Bold moves and fast opportunities; act decisively but don't skip the details.
mood: Adventurous and impulsive — hot energy that wants to move now.
spiritual: Passion as fuel: honoring the fire while learning to ground it so it warms rather than burns.

name: Queen of Wands
numerology: Queen — the nurturer: inward mastery of the suit
keywords_upright: confidence; warmth; determination; magnetism; independence
keywords_reversed: insecurity; jealousy; demanding nature; burnout; dimmed confidence
meaning_upright: The Queen of Wands is warm, confident, and magnetic — someone who owns a room without demanding it.
meaning_reversed: The Queen's fire turns inward or sour: insecurity behind bravado, jealousy, or warmth depleted by overextension.
love: Passionate, self-assured energy — attraction rooted in independence rather than need.
career: Confident leadership and creative drive that inspires others.
mood: Confident and magnetic — warmth that fills a room.
spiritual: She is authentic radiance: self-expression as a form of generosity, shining without apology.

name: King of Wands
numerology: King — the master: outward authority over the suit
keywords_upright: visionary leadership; boldness; entrepreneurship; mastery of fire; inspiration
keywords_reversed: arrogance; tyranny; impulsive leadership; unfulfilled vision; domineering
meaning_upright: The King of Wands is the visionary leader: fire mastered and directed at long-range goals.
meaning_reversed: The King becomes domineering or erratic — vision without patience, authority without listening.
love: A confident, protective partner with big plans — generous, though he must share the wheel.
career: Entrepreneurial vision and decisive leadership — think big and commit fully.
mood: Bold and visionary — leadership energy, eyes on the horizon.
spiritual: He is purpose embodied: directing creative fire toward a vision larger than the self.

name: Ace of Cups
keywords_upright: new love; emotional beginning; compassion; overflowing heart; spiritual opening
keywords_reversed: blocked emotions; emptiness; repressed feelings; emotional loss; self-love needed
meaning_upright: The Ace of Cups is the heart overflowing: new love, deep compassion, or an emotional and spiritual awakening.
meaning_reversed: The Ace shows the cup tipped or blocked — emotions withheld, love unexpressed, or an inner emptiness asking for attention.
love: The strongest cards for new romance or a fresh emotional depth in an existing bond.
career: Emotionally fulfilling work, creative flow, or warm new connections.
mood: Tender and open-hearted — feelings close to the surface, in the best way.
spiritual: The Ace of Cups is love as opening: the heart offered and refilled from a source deeper than circumstance.

name: Two of Cups
keywords_upright: partnership; mutual attraction; connection; harmony; union of equals
keywords_reversed: imbalance; broken connection; tension; miscommunication; self-partnership needed
meaning_upright: The Two of Cups is mutual recognition: two people meeting as equals in attraction, friendship, or alliance.
meaning_reversed: A connection out of balance — one cup fuller than the other, trust strained, or harmony disrupted.
love: Mutual attraction and partnership — a bond of genuine reciprocity.
career: Partnerships, collaborations, and one-on-one alliances built on mutual respect.
mood: Affectionate and harmonious — met, mirrored, understood.
spiritual: Sacred connection: another person as a mirror in which the soul recognizes itself.

name: Three of Cups
keywords_upright: celebration; friendship; community; joy shared; creative collaboration
keywords_reversed: overindulgence; third-party interference; isolation from friends; gossip; excess
meaning_upright: The Three of Cups is friendship and shared joy — the toast among people who genuinely celebrate each other.
meaning_reversed: Overindulgence, gossip, or a third presence straining a bond.
love: Celebration and social warmth, though reversed it can flag outside interference in the relationship.
career: Successful teamwork and collaborative wins worth celebrating.
mood: Joyful and sociable — laughter shared, cups raised.
spiritual: Communion: gratitude multiplied by being celebrated among kindred spirits.

name: Four of Cups
keywords_upright: apathy; contemplation; disconnection; missed offer; reevaluation
keywords_reversed: renewed interest; accepting the offer; emerging from withdrawal; new motivation; openness
meaning_upright: The Four of Cups is emotional weariness: arms crossed, absorbed in discontent, while a new cup is offered unseen.
meaning_reversed: The end of apathy — noticing the offered cup at last and re-engaging with life.
love: Emotional distance or taking a partner for granted; an offer of connection may be going unnoticed.
career: Disengagement or an overlooked opportunity — look again before saying nothing interests you.
mood: Apathetic and withdrawn — full cups nearby, appetite gone.
spiritual: A call to presence: noticing the blessing being offered before deciding nothing is there.

name: Five of Cups
keywords_upright: loss; grief; regret; focusing on what's gone; disappointment
keywords_reversed: acceptance; moving on; forgiveness; seeing what remains; healing
meaning_upright: The Five of Cups is grief fixed on the three spilled cups while two still stand full behind.
meaning_reversed: The turn toward healing: accepting what happened, forgiving, and finally seeing the cups still standing.
love: Grieving a loss or disappointment — and to the remaining connection that grief may be obscuring.
career: A setback worth mourning briefly, then salvaging — something valuable survived the spill.
mood: Sorrowful and regretful — attention fixed on what spilled.
spiritual: Grief as teacher: mourning honestly, then turning to the cups that still stand.

name: Six of Cups
keywords_upright: nostalgia; childhood; innocence; reunion; simple kindness
keywords_reversed: stuck in the past; rose-tinted memory; leaving home; growing up; releasing old ties
meaning_upright: The Six of Cups is sweet memory and simple kindness — nostalgia, reunions, and the healing comfort of the familiar.
meaning_reversed: Living in a rose-tinted past at the present's expense.
love: Innocent affection, a past lover resurfacing, or reconnecting with why you fell for each other.
career: Returning to earlier passions or working with something from your past.
mood: Nostalgic and sweet — memory softening the present.
spiritual: Inner-child healing: revisiting innocence to reclaim the simple kindness the soul remembers.

name: Seven of Cups
keywords_upright: choices; fantasy; illusion; wishful thinking; options
keywords_reversed: clarity; decision made; cutting through illusion; overwhelm ending; realism
meaning_upright: The Seven of Cups is a cloud of glittering options, some real and some mirage.
meaning_reversed: The fog lifts: illusions dissolve, priorities sharpen, and a real choice gets made.
love: Idealizing someone or keeping options endlessly open instead of choosing.
career: Many possibilities but a need for realistic evaluation — pick one and commit.
mood: Dreamy and indecisive — many doors, all glittering.
spiritual: Discernment: asking which vision feeds the soul and which merely fascinates it.

name: Eight of Cups
keywords_upright: walking away; seeking deeper meaning; leaving behind; disillusionment; quest
keywords_reversed: fear of leaving; returning; avoidance; staying too long; unfinished departure
meaning_upright: The Eight of Cups is the quiet departure: leaving behind something built because it no longer feeds the soul.
meaning_reversed: The departure stalled — knowing it's time to go but staying, or drifting away without truly deciding.
love: Emotionally outgrowing a situation and the difficult choice to walk away or recommit fully.
career: Leaving a stable but unfulfilling path in search of meaningful work.
mood: Wistful and restless — the quiet ache of outgrowing something.
spiritual: The soul's quest: leaving the comfortable shore because meaning has moved elsewhere.

name: Nine of Cups
keywords_upright: wishes fulfilled; contentment; satisfaction; pleasure; gratitude
keywords_reversed: superficial happiness; greed; unfulfilled wishes; smugness; inner lack
meaning_upright: The Nine of Cups is the wish card: satisfaction, comfort, and desires coming to fruition.
meaning_reversed: The satisfaction on display — pleasure that doesn't nourish, smugness, or wishes granted that weren't the right wishes.
love: Emotional contentment and wishes in matters of the heart coming true.
career: Well-earned satisfaction and rewards — enjoy the achievement without coasting.
mood: Content and satisfied — wishes landing, comfort earned.
spiritual: Gratitude's test: enjoying fulfillment while asking what truly nourishes beneath the pleasure.

name: Ten of Cups
numerology: 10 — completion of a cycle; reduces to 1, a new beginning
keywords_upright: lasting happiness; family harmony; emotional fulfillment; love realized; belonging
keywords_reversed: family discord; broken harmony; unrealistic ideals; disconnection at home; strained bonds
meaning_upright: The Ten of Cups is the rainbow over the family: lasting emotional fulfillment, harmony at home, and love realized in daily life.
meaning_reversed: The picture and the reality diverging — tension behind the family portrait or ideals straining real relationships.
love: Deep commitment, family, and shared happiness — the full emotional arc.
career: Work-life harmony and an environment that feels like belonging.
mood: Loving and fulfilled — harmony that feels like home.
spiritual: Love realized: everyday life itself becoming the blessing that was once only wished for.

name: Page of Cups
numerology: Page — the student: curiosity, learning, messages
keywords_upright: emotional openness; creative message; intuition budding; sensitivity; sweet surprise
keywords_reversed: emotional immaturity; moodiness; blocked creativity; escapism; oversensitivity
meaning_upright: The Page of Cups is the tender dreamer bearing an unexpected gift — a sweet message, a creative impulse, or a first stirring of feeling.
meaning_reversed: The Page turns moody or escapist: feelings expressed sideways, creativity blocked, or sensitivity tipping into sulking.
love: Sweet gestures, budding feelings, or a sensitive admirer.
career: Creative beginnings and intuitive nudges worth taking seriously.
mood: Playful and sensitive — imagination bubbling up unannounced.
spiritual: Budding intuition: whimsical messages from the heart that deserve to be taken seriously.

name: Knight of Cups
numerology: Knight — the quester: action, pursuit, dedication
keywords_upright: romance; charm; invitation; following the heart; idealism
keywords_reversed: unrealistic promises; moodiness; disillusionment; flattery; heart over head gone wrong
meaning_upright: The Knight of Cups rides in with an offer of the heart — romance, an invitation, or an ideal pursued with genuine feeling.
meaning_reversed: The Knight's charm outpaces his substance: beautiful words without follow-through, or idealism curdling into disappointment.
love: He is the classic romantic — courtship, proposals, and grand feeling — though reversed his promises need testing.
career: Creative proposals and appealing offers; verify the practical details.
mood: Romantic and idealistic — led by the heart, gladly.
spiritual: The heart's calling: following feeling as a compass while keeping one foot on the earth.

name: Queen of Cups
numerology: Queen — the nurturer: inward mastery of the suit
keywords_upright: compassion; emotional depth; intuition; nurturing presence; empathy
keywords_reversed: emotional overwhelm; codependency; martyrdom; boundaries dissolved; manipulation through feeling
meaning_upright: The Queen of Cups is deep water held in a graceful vessel: profound empathy, intuition, and emotional wisdom.
meaning_reversed: The Queen loses her shoreline — absorbing everyone's feelings, giving past empty, or using emotion to steer others.
love: Emotional attunement and devoted care — a bond where feelings are truly heard.
career: Empathic leadership, counseling, and work guided by emotional intelligence.
mood: Empathic and calm — deep waters, gently held.
spiritual: She is compassion embodied: emotional depth as a gift that heals others without drowning the self.

name: King of Cups
numerology: King — the master: outward authority over the suit
keywords_upright: emotional mastery; calm authority; diplomacy; balance of heart and head; wise counsel
keywords_reversed: repressed emotion; coldness; manipulation; moodiness in power; emotional volatility
meaning_upright: The King of Cups sails turbulent seas without capsizing: emotional mastery, diplomacy, and compassion held in steady balance.
meaning_reversed: The King's calm is a lid: repressed feelings leaking as coldness, moodiness, or quiet manipulation.
love: He is the emotionally steady partner — supportive and composed, provided the depths are shared, not sealed.
career: Wise counsel and diplomacy — staying gracious and level under pressure.
mood: Steady and gracious — feelings present, never in charge.
spiritual: He is mastery of the heart: wisdom that comes from feeling everything and being ruled by none of it.

name: Ace of Swords
keywords_upright: clarity; breakthrough; truth; new idea; mental sharpness
keywords_reversed: confusion; clouded judgment; misused intellect; harsh words; false clarity
meaning_upright: The Ace of Swords is a breakthrough of clarity: the truth cutting cleanly through confusion, a decisive new idea, or a moment of piercing insight.
meaning_reversed: The blade is dulled or misaimed — muddled thinking, arguments that wound without resolving, or a 'truth' built on faulty premises.
love: Honest conversation — clarity about feelings and intentions, even when it's uncomfortable.
career: A breakthrough idea or decisive clarity about direction — act on the insight.
mood: Sharp and alert — the mind cutting cleanly through fog.
spiritual: The Ace of Swords is revelation: truth arriving like light, asking to be spoken with care.

name: Two of Swords
keywords_upright: stalemate; difficult choice; avoidance; blocked feelings; truce
keywords_reversed: decision forced; information revealed; stalemate breaking; anxiety; release of denial
meaning_upright: The Two of Swords is a self-imposed stalemate: blindfolded, arms crossed, refusing to look at the choice that must be made.
meaning_reversed: The blindfold slips: the deadlock breaks, information surfaces, or the choice is forced by events.
love: Avoidance of a hard conversation or a heart guarded behind crossed swords.
career: A decision being postponed — gather the missing facts and choose.
mood: Torn and guarded — a standstill held with effort.
spiritual: Stillness before decision: closing the eyes to the noise so inner counsel can be heard.

name: Three of Swords
keywords_upright: heartbreak; painful truth; grief; sorrow; separation
keywords_reversed: healing heartbreak; forgiveness; releasing pain; recovery; old wounds resurfacing
meaning_upright: The Three of Swords is heartbreak rendered honestly: a painful truth, betrayal, or loss that pierces cleanly.
meaning_reversed: The slow removal of the swords: forgiveness, healing, and pain beginning to release — though sometimes it flags an old wound reopening to finally heal properly.
love: Heartbreak, hard truths, or separation — grief that must be felt before it can pass.
career: A painful disappointment or harsh feedback that, once absorbed, clarifies the path.
mood: Hurt and raw — a truth that stings.
spiritual: Heartbreak as opening: pain that names exactly what needs healing, so healing can begin.

name: Four of Swords
keywords_upright: rest; recovery; retreat; stillness; recharging
keywords_reversed: burnout; restlessness; forced rest; returning to action; exhaustion ignored
meaning_upright: The Four of Swords is sanctioned rest: a deliberate retreat to recover before the next battle.
meaning_reversed: Rest refused becomes rest imposed — burnout, illness, or collapse.
love: A pause to breathe and reflect rather than pushing the relationship's questions right now.
career: Genuine downtime and recovery before performance suffers.
mood: Quiet and tired — the system asking for stillness.
spiritual: Rest as sacred practice: retreat, meditation, and silence as the soul's repair.

name: Five of Swords
keywords_upright: hollow victory; conflict; winning at all costs; discord; self-interest
keywords_reversed: reconciliation; making amends; releasing resentment; lingering grudges; lesson learned
meaning_upright: The Five of Swords is the victory that costs more than it wins: the argument won, the relationship damaged.
meaning_reversed: It opens the door to reconciliation — apologies, dropped grudges, and the choice to repair over re-fighting.
love: Fights where winning the point loses the person — pride is the real opponent.
career: It flags cutthroat dynamics or a win that burns bridges; weigh the long-term cost.
mood: Bitter and conflicted — a win that doesn't feel like one.
spiritual: Choosing peace over victory: releasing the need to be right in favor of what is whole.

name: Six of Swords
keywords_upright: transition; moving on; calmer waters; passage; gradual healing
keywords_reversed: resisting transition; unfinished business; rough crossing; returning to trouble; stuck baggage
meaning_upright: The Six of Swords is the quiet ferry to calmer waters: leaving difficulty behind and moving, gradually, toward relief.
meaning_reversed: The crossing stalls: baggage too heavy to load, a return to the troubled shore, or transition resisted out of fear.
love: Moving past a rough patch together, or transitioning out of a relationship toward peace.
career: A transition — new role, new environment — that trades turbulence for steadier progress.
mood: Subdued but relieved — moving away from rough water.
spiritual: Passage: carrying the lessons while letting the shore of suffering recede.

name: Seven of Swords
keywords_upright: deception; strategy; acting alone; getting away with it; cunning
keywords_reversed: confession; conscience; exposure; coming clean; self-deception revealed
meaning_upright: The Seven of Swords is the thief tiptoeing from camp: deception, shortcuts, or a strategy that avoids direct confrontation.
meaning_reversed: The sneaking ends: secrets surface, conscience speaks, or the deceiver — sometimes oneself — is caught.
love: Hidden agendas, secrets, or avoidance of honest confrontation.
career: It flags office politics, cut corners, or ideas taken without credit — protect your work and play straight.
mood: Wary and calculating — something not quite in the open.
spiritual: Honesty with self: noticing where cleverness hides avoidance and choosing the direct path.

name: Eight of Swords
keywords_upright: feeling trapped; self-restriction; victim mindset; mental prison; paralysis
keywords_reversed: liberation; new perspective; self-empowerment; blindfold removed; taking responsibility
meaning_upright: The Eight of Swords is a prison of thought: bound and blindfolded among swords, yet the bindings are loose and the path is clear.
meaning_reversed: The blindfold comes off: recognizing one's own role in the trap and stepping free of it.
love: Feeling stuck in a dynamic that beliefs, not circumstances, are maintaining.
career: Feeling trapped in a role — the constraints deserve testing, most are assumptions.
mood: Anxious and stuck — trapped by thoughts more than facts.
spiritual: Freeing the mind: seeing that most cages are beliefs, and beliefs can be set down.

name: Nine of Swords
keywords_upright: anxiety; sleepless nights; worry; dread; mental anguish
keywords_reversed: easing anxiety; facing fears; seeking help; perspective returning; recovery from despair
meaning_upright: The Nine of Swords is the 3 a.m.
meaning_reversed: The night begins to end: fears voiced lose their size, help is accepted, and perspective returns with daylight.
love: It reflects anxiety and worst-case spirals about the relationship — check fears against reality, ideally out loud.
career: Stress and dread outpacing the actual situation; name the worry and it shrinks.
mood: Distressed and sleepless — worry loud in the dark.
spiritual: Meeting fear with compassion: naming the dread aloud so daylight can right-size it.

name: Ten of Swords
numerology: 10 — completion of a cycle; reduces to 1, a new beginning
keywords_upright: painful ending; rock bottom; betrayal; collapse; the worst is over
keywords_reversed: recovery; rising again; surviving the ending; old wounds closing; refusing the final lesson
meaning_upright: The Ten of Swords is the definitive ending — dramatic, painful, and complete.
meaning_reversed: Survival and slow rising after collapse — or a refusal to let something already dead finally end.
love: A painful but final ending or betrayal; the closure, however harsh, frees what comes next.
career: A project or role collapsing completely — salvage the lesson and rebuild elsewhere.
mood: Defeated and drained — an ending felt completely.
spiritual: Surrender and rebirth: lying down with what is over so the sunrise can begin something new.

name: Page of Swords
numerology: Page — the student: curiosity, learning, messages
keywords_upright: curiosity; vigilance; new ideas; mental energy; truth-seeking
keywords_reversed: gossip; hasty words; scattered thinking; spying; all talk
meaning_upright: The Page of Swords is restless intellect on watch: curious, quick, asking sharp questions and guarding against deception.
meaning_reversed: The Page's sharpness cuts sideways — gossip, snooping, hasty conclusions, or big talk without study behind it.
love: Probing questions and honest talk, though reversed it warns of suspicion or gossip corroding trust.
career: Research, due diligence, and fresh ideas — verify before broadcasting.
mood: Inquisitive and alert — questions flying, eyes open.
spiritual: Truth-seeking: the watchful mind as a young guardian of honesty.

name: Knight of Swords
numerology: Knight — the quester: action, pursuit, dedication
keywords_upright: decisive action; ambition; directness; charging ahead; intellectual drive
keywords_reversed: recklessness; aggression; rushing past details; burnout sprint; verbal attacks
meaning_upright: The Knight of Swords charges at full gallop toward the goal — fast, focused, and utterly direct.
meaning_reversed: Speed becomes recklessness: bulldozed feelings, skipped details, and words used as weapons.
love: Intense, fast-moving pursuit — exciting but potentially inconsiderate of a partner's pace.
career: Bold, rapid execution of a clear plan; guard against steamrolling colleagues.
mood: Intense and hasty — thought turned to charge.
spiritual: Focus tempered: aiming the mind's speed so conviction doesn't outrun wisdom.

name: Queen of Swords
numerology: Queen — the nurturer: inward mastery of the suit
keywords_upright: clear judgment; independence; honest communication; boundaries; experienced wisdom
keywords_reversed: coldness; bitterness; harsh criticism; isolation; cynicism
meaning_upright: The Queen of Swords sees through pretense with compassionate precision: honest, independent, and wise through experience — including painful experience.
meaning_reversed: Her clarity hardens into cynicism: criticism without warmth, walls without doors.
love: Honesty and independence — genuine connection must respect her boundaries and intelligence.
career: Sharp judgment and fair, direct leadership — say the true thing kindly.
mood: Clear and composed — no illusions, no malice.
spiritual: She is discernment: truth delivered with compassion, boundaries held with grace.

name: King of Swords
numerology: King — the master: outward authority over the suit
keywords_upright: intellectual authority; truth; impartial judgment; strategy; ethical clarity
keywords_reversed: abuse of intellect; cold ruthlessness; manipulation; rigid logic; unjust judgment
meaning_upright: The King of Swords rules by clear thought and fair law: impartial, articulate, and strategically brilliant.
meaning_reversed: Intellect turns tyrant — logic weaponized, rules bent to serve power, judgment without mercy.
love: Rational steadiness that must remember to speak the heart's language too.
career: Authoritative expertise, legal or strategic matters, and decisions that must be scrupulously fair.
mood: Rational and authoritative — thought ordered, judgment calm.
spiritual: He is the ethical mind: clarity in service of fairness, intellect answering to principle.

name: Ace of Pentacles
keywords_upright: new opportunity; prosperity; manifestation; solid start; material gift
keywords_reversed: missed opportunity; shaky foundation; scarcity thinking; delayed reward; poor investment
meaning_upright: The Ace of Pentacles is a tangible new opportunity — a seed of prosperity offered from a steady hand.
meaning_reversed: The seed slips away or lands on poor soil: an opportunity missed, delayed, or built on shaky ground.
love: A relationship with real-world staying power — grounded, stable, and worth investing in.
career: A concrete opportunity — new job, raise, or venture — with solid long-term potential.
mood: Hopeful and grounded — opportunity that feels solid in the hand.
spiritual: The Ace of Pentacles is the seed of abundance: matter as a place where spirit takes root.

name: Two of Pentacles
keywords_upright: juggling priorities; adaptability; balance in motion; flexibility; managing change
keywords_reversed: overcommitment; dropped balls; disorganization; financial strain; overwhelm
meaning_upright: The Two of Pentacles juggles competing demands with a dancer's rhythm — work and home, income and expense, this and that.
meaning_reversed: The juggle fails: too many commitments, dropped responsibilities, or finances wobbling.
love: Balancing the relationship against life's other demands — presence needs scheduling too.
career: Multitasking and shifting priorities; adaptability works until it becomes overload.
mood: Busy and adaptable — juggling kept just barely graceful.
spiritual: Balance as practice: finding rhythm within motion instead of waiting for stillness.

name: Three of Pentacles
keywords_upright: teamwork; craftsmanship; skill recognized; collaboration; building together
keywords_reversed: poor teamwork; mediocrity; lack of recognition; misaligned goals; working alone
meaning_upright: The Three of Pentacles is mastery meeting collaboration: skilled work recognized and improved by working with others.
meaning_reversed: The collaboration frays — credit disputed, standards slipping, or everyone building a different cathedral.
love: The relationship as a joint project — effort, communication, and shared building.
career: Successful teamwork and skill being noticed by people who matter.
mood: Cooperative and engaged — skill meeting appreciation.
spiritual: Craft as devotion: building carefully with others as a quiet form of worship.

name: Four of Pentacles
keywords_upright: security; holding on; control; saving; possessiveness
keywords_reversed: letting go; generosity; loosening control; financial release; spending
meaning_upright: The Four of Pentacles clutches what it has: security achieved, then gripped so tightly nothing new can be received.
meaning_reversed: The grip opens — generosity, wise spending, or releasing control that was strangling growth.
love: Possessiveness or emotional withholding — safety that comes from control isn't intimacy.
career: Financial caution and consolidation, with a caution against hoarding resources or authority.
mood: Cautious and guarded — holding on a little too tightly.
spiritual: Loosening attachment: learning that security gripped becomes a cage, and released becomes trust.

name: Five of Pentacles
keywords_upright: hardship; scarcity; feeling left out in the cold; financial loss; isolation
keywords_reversed: recovery; help accepted; hardship ending; finding shelter; renewed hope
meaning_upright: The Five of Pentacles is the cold outside the lit window: material hardship, exclusion, or the feeling that help is for other people.
meaning_reversed: The hard season turns: help is accepted, finances stabilize, and the sense of exile eases.
love: It reflects feeling shut out or weathering hardship as a couple — let the other person in from the cold.
career: Financial strain or job insecurity; seek the available support rather than enduring alone.
mood: Discouraged and cold — outside the lit window, for now.
spiritual: Faith through hardship: remembering the door is unlocked and letting help in is not failure.

name: Six of Pentacles
keywords_upright: generosity; giving and receiving; support; fairness; charity
keywords_reversed: strings attached; power imbalance; one-sided giving; debt; self-neglect in giving
meaning_upright: The Six of Pentacles is the flow of generosity in balance: giving from abundance, receiving with grace, and the scales held fair.
meaning_reversed: The gift has hooks: charity with strings, loans that become leverage, or giving that quietly buys control.
love: It asks whether giving and receiving are balanced — care should circulate, not flow one way.
career: Mentorship, raises, or support networks — generosity now returns multiplied.
mood: Generous and balanced — giving and receiving in easy rhythm.
spiritual: The sacred flow: abundance circulating, blessing the giver and receiver alike.

name: Seven of Pentacles
keywords_upright: patience; assessment; long-term view; slow growth; waiting for harvest
keywords_reversed: impatience; wasted effort; poor returns; giving up early; misdirected work
meaning_upright: The Seven of Pentacles leans on the hoe and evaluates the vines: effort invested, growth visible, harvest not yet in.
meaning_reversed: The investment: effort poured into thin soil, impatience uprooting what needed time, or persistence in a field that will never yield.
love: A pause to assess the relationship's growth — investment is judged by trajectory, not just time served.
career: Long-term projects maturing slowly; evaluate returns honestly before investing further.
mood: Patient and evaluative — leaning on the hoe, taking stock.
spiritual: Trust in slow growth: honoring seasons that ripen quietly beneath the surface.

name: Eight of Pentacles
keywords_upright: diligence; skill-building; craftsmanship; dedication; mastery through repetition
keywords_reversed: perfectionism; tedium; cut corners; misplaced effort; burnout from grind
meaning_upright: The Eight of Pentacles is the apprentice at the bench, carving pentacle after pentacle a little better each time.
meaning_reversed: The work sours: perfectionism that never ships, corners cut from boredom, or diligence spent on skills that lead nowhere wanted.
love: Connection as craft — small consistent efforts, done attentively, build the bond.
career: Skill development and dedicated work — the mastery being built now compounds.
mood: Diligent and absorbed — good work, done with care.
spiritual: Work as meditation: mastery built one mindful repetition at a time.

name: Nine of Pentacles
keywords_upright: self-sufficiency; earned luxury; independence; refinement; enjoying success
keywords_reversed: overdependence; hollow success; overwork without enjoyment; financial insecurity; self-worth tied to wealth
meaning_upright: The Nine of Pentacles stands in a garden of her own making: independence, refinement, and abundance earned by discipline.
meaning_reversed: The garden loses its pleasure: success that feels hollow, dependence dressed as luxury, or working so hard for the vineyard there's no time to taste the grapes.
love: Wholeness over neediness — a partnership of two complete people, or contentment in one's own company.
career: Financial independence and well-earned rewards; your work stands on its own merit.
mood: Self-assured and serene — enjoying a garden of one's own making.
spiritual: Gratitude for earned abundance: independence held lightly, savored fully.

name: Ten of Pentacles
numerology: 10 — completion of a cycle; reduces to 1, a new beginning
keywords_upright: legacy; lasting wealth; family security; inheritance; long-term foundations
keywords_reversed: family disputes over resources; unstable foundations; short-term thinking; lost inheritance; wealth without belonging
meaning_upright: The Ten of Pentacles is wealth that outlives its maker: family security, legacy, and structures built to last generations.
meaning_reversed: The estate shakes: family friction over money, foundations less solid than they look, or riches that never became a home.
love: Commitment with deep roots — family, shared assets, a future measured in decades.
career: Lasting success, established institutions, and decisions best made on a generational timescale.
mood: Secure and proud — roots deep, table full.
spiritual: Legacy: blessings tended so well they outlive the hands that planted them.

name: Page of Pentacles
numerology: Page — the student: curiosity, learning, messages
keywords_upright: studiousness; new practical skill; opportunity to learn; grounded ambition; diligent beginnings
keywords_reversed: procrastination; lack of progress; daydreaming without doing; short-lived focus; missed lessons
meaning_upright: The Page of Pentacles studies the coin like a scholar with a fascinating text: eager to learn, practical, and quietly ambitious.
meaning_reversed: The studying stalls: plans admired but not started, focus that evaporates, lessons available but unlearned.
love: Sincere, steady interest that builds slowly and values reliability over fireworks.
career: Learning opportunities, new practical ventures, and ambitions pursued step by step.
mood: Studious and motivated — a plan taking earnest shape.
spiritual: Grounded manifestation: treating study and small steps as the honest magic they are.

name: Knight of Pentacles
numerology: Knight — the quester: action, pursuit, dedication
keywords_upright: reliability; methodical progress; hard work; patience; commitment to routine
keywords_reversed: stagnation; boredom; stubbornness; workaholism; stuck in a rut
meaning_upright: The Knight of Pentacles is the slowest knight and the only one who always arrives: methodical, reliable, and immune to shortcuts.
meaning_reversed: Steadiness becomes stagnation: routine hardened into rut, diligence into drudgery, patience into refusal to adapt.
love: He is the dependable partner who shows up every time — devotion expressed through consistency rather than drama.
career: Slow, thorough progress and trustworthy execution; the tortoise wins this one.
mood: Methodical and steady — unhurried, unstoppable.
spiritual: Devotion through consistency: showing up daily as its own quiet vow.

name: Queen of Pentacles
numerology: Queen — the nurturer: inward mastery of the suit
keywords_upright: nurturing practicality; resourcefulness; warmth and security; working provider; down-to-earth care
keywords_reversed: self-neglect; work-home imbalance; smothering through providing; financial codependence; depleted caregiver
meaning_upright: The Queen of Pentacles keeps a warm, well-run world: nurturing expressed through practical care — the meal cooked, the budget balanced, the garden thriving.
meaning_reversed: The provider runs dry: caring for everyone but herself, juggling work and home past sustainability, or measuring love in provisions.
love: Steady, tangible devotion — a home built together, care shown in daily acts.
career: Resourceful management and creating environments where people and projects flourish.
mood: Nurturing and practical — warmth expressed in tangible care.
spiritual: She is the sacred ordinary: body, home, and hearth tended as holy ground.

name: King of Pentacles
numerology: King — the master: outward authority over the suit
keywords_upright: material mastery; abundance; steady leadership; business acumen; reliable provider
keywords_reversed: greed; materialism; rigid control of resources; status obsession; corruption of comfort
meaning_upright: The King of Pentacles rules a prosperous, well-tended realm: business mastery, patient wealth-building, and generosity from genuine abundance.
meaning_reversed: The golden touch corrupts: wealth as scoreboard, control of resources as power over people, comfort defended at any cost.
love: He is the committed provider whose loyalty is bedrock — warmth must flow alongside the security he builds.
career: The summit of material success — sound investments, business leadership, and enduring prosperity.
mood: Assured and generous — abundance worn comfortably.
spiritual: He is stewardship: wealth as responsibility, prosperity shared as gratitude made visible.
